free_care.notes: Uninsured family income ≤200% FPL at first billing qualifies for full charity care.

high_medical_cost.notes: Insured patients 0–400% FPL pay a sliding-scale share of the patient portion (Table 2): <200%=40%, 201–250%=60%, 251–400%=80%; combined payments capped at greater of Medicare/Medi-Cal.

discount_payment.notes: Uninsured 201–400% FPL pay a % of what Medicare would have paid (Table 1 sliding scale).

payment_plans.notes: Qualified Payment Plan carries no interest; falls back to HSC §127400(i) 'reasonable payment plan' if no agreement.

eligibility_process.presumptive_criteria: Homeless patients; deceased without third-party coverage/estate/probate; certain declared categories qualify presumptively.

excluded_services: Separately-billed physician fees (e.g. ER physicians, radiologists)
scope.notes: ER physician services to uninsured/high-medical-cost patients ≤400% FPL are discounted per state law, billed separately.

additional_provisions: Patients >400% FPL with a catastrophic medical event may qualify at hospital management's discretion, based on liability at billed charges plus income/assets.
Patient 'family' defined per age (spouse/domestic partner/dependent children <21, or parent/caretaker for minors).

source_quotes.free_care: If an uninsured patient's family income is 200% or less of the FPL ... the patient qualifies for full charity care.
source_quotes.discount_ceiling: If an uninsured patient's family income is between 201% and 400% of the FPL ...

extraction_notes: Two distinct sliding scales: uninsured (Table 1, % of Medicare) vs insured patient-portion (Table 2). Both captured.
